narrative_summary: The engine compared expected and actual outcomes, then mapped the biggest deltas to recurring audience, creative, and platform signals. Positive metric movement is treated as a repeatable pattern candidate, while cost inflation or missing revenue is flagged for follow-up.
key_learnings: CTR beat forecast by 75.0%, indicating stronger message-market fit than planned.
CVR improved by 4.6%, suggesting the landing journey and offer matched traffic quality.
18-24 audiences drive a 67% stronger performance score versus baseline, with 24% lower CVR and 49% lower CPA across 2 campaigns.
18-25 audiences drive a 53% stronger performance score versus baseline, with 22% lower CVR and 47% lower CPA across 4 campaigns.
Reel creatives deliver a 67% stronger performance score, 15% lower CVR, and 55% lower CPA while sustaining 3.5% CTR across 1 campaigns.
Ugc_Video creatives deliver a 67% stronger performance score, 32% lower CVR, and 43% lower CPA while sustaining 3.5% CTR across 1 campaigns.
recommendations: Expand tests around `audience:18-24` and preserve the audience framing.
Expand tests around `audience:18-25` and preserve the audience framing.
Tighten budget guardrails and require evidence before scaling this pattern.
Reuse the playbook from `cmp_001_meta_genz_video_launch` because it is the nearest semantic match in memory.
anomalies: cmp_005_linkedin_exec_report is overspending relative to history, with CPA at 105.56 on 7600.00 spend while only converting at 9.5%.
cmp_002_google_search_saas_demo is overspending relative to history, with CPA at 67.71 on 6500.00 spend while only converting at 4.2%.
